description: In Go, run the package-local validation gate (go build ./... with the module's build tags and GOOS/GOARCH) and summarize the diagnostics.
body: In Go, this capability runs the owning package's gate and reports the outcome so the next action is obvious. Use when you need an authoritative pass/fail for a Go package before or after a change. Do not run a root or generic command that bypasses the package's real config, and avoid reporting success without the exit code. Actions: take the package path, run go vet ./... and the compiler's unused-variable/import errors then go vet, staticcheck, and the race detector (go test -race) and go test ./... -race on the changed package, and record the command, exit code, the first compiler or vet message with package path and line, and the recommended next step. Validation signals include the captured command log, the exit code, and the first diagnostics with file and line. Risk boundary: surface, do not auto-fix; escalate when the gate requires network access, credentials, or a toolchain change.
